entity.summary: Global centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2017. HEI records CoinEx as an active exchange with event-backed history, including a 2023 New York regulatory settlement and a 2023 hot-wallet security breach.
entity.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0550 and hei_unadded_0551 after separate event-backed review. CoinEx remains active, so this is not a shutdown record. The 2023 hot-wallet breach event should be improved later with stronger primary or archived official-source evidence.

events.title: CoinEx agreed to New York ban and monetary settlement
events.description: CoinEx agreed to pay about $1.8 million and be banned from operating in New York to resolve a lawsuit brought by the New York Attorney General alleging illegal unregistered operations in the state.
events.notes: This is a jurisdiction-specific regulatory action and does not mark CoinEx as dead globally.

events.title: CoinEx reported hot-wallet security breach
events.description: CoinEx reported a hot-wallet security incident in September 2023; third-party reporting and summaries described losses of roughly $70 million and suspected Lazarus Group attribution by blockchain analysts.
events.notes: Security breach details should be strengthened later with primary CoinEx incident posts or archived forensic reports if available.

events.title: CoinEx verified as active exchange candidate
events.description: CoinEx is consolidated from CoinPaprika and CoinGecko candidate rows into one active centralized exchange record.
events.notes: Consumes database candidate rows hei_unadded_0550 and hei_unadded_0551.

evidence.title: CoinEx accepts New York ban, to pay $1.8 million to resolve attorney general lawsuit
evidence.notes: Reuters report on the New York settlement and ban.

evidence.title: New York Attorney General CoinEx settlement reference
evidence.notes: Regulator-side settlement reference. URL should be rechecked if the press-release slug changes.

evidence.title: Blockchain analysts suspect N. Korea-linked hackers behind $70 mln crypto theft
evidence.notes: Report cited in secondary reference material for the CoinEx September 2023 hot-wallet breach.

evidence.title: CoinEx secondary summary reference
evidence.notes: Temporary secondary reference for breach summary. Replace with official CoinEx incident note, forensic report, or primary news source when available.

evidence.title: CoinEx official website
evidence.notes: Official domain used to verify active identity.

evidence.notes: Source used by verified-unadded backlog row hei_unadded_0550.
